meaning: to lower in rank or dignity; humiliate
examples: The king was abased after being captured by his enemies.
She felt abased when her boss criticized her in front of the whole team.
mnemonic: A-BASE: Hit rock BASE, totally HUMILIATED

meaning: to become less intense or widespread
examples: The storm finally abated after hours of heavy rain.
His anger began to abate once he heard the full explanation.
mnemonic: A-BATE: The BATE (bat) flew away, storm DECREASED

meaning: to give up power or responsibility
examples: The king chose to abdicate the throne rather than face a revolution.
Parents should never abdicate their responsibility to guide their children.
mnemonic: ABDI-CATE: Like a cat, he walked away from the throne

meaning: to take someone away by force
examples: The criminals attempted to abduct the wealthy businessman's daughter.
In the movie, aliens abduct people from small towns.
mnemonic: AB-DUCT: DUCT tape over mouth = KIDNAP

meaning: something that differs from what is normal
examples: His poor test score was an aberration; he usually gets straight A's.
The sudden snowstorm in July was a strange aberration.
mnemonic: A-BEAR-RATION: A bear in your house is NOT NORMAL

meaning: to help or encourage someone to do something wrong
examples: By lending him the car, she unwittingly abetted his criminal plan.
The lawyer was accused of abetting fraud by hiding evidence.
mnemonic: A-BET: Place a BET to ENCOURAGE gambling

examples: I abhor cruelty to animals and will always speak out against it.
She abhors dishonesty and refuses to work with people who lie.

meaning: 1. to accept or follow; 2. to stay or remain
examples: All players must abide by the rules of the game.
Despite the challenges, their friendship will abide through the years.
mnemonic: A-BIDE: BIDE your time, REMAIN patient

examples: After losing everything in the fire, they lived in abject poverty.
His abject apology showed how truly sorry he was.

meaning: to formally reject or give up a belief
examples: He abjured his former political views after learning the truth.
The witness was asked to abjure her earlier false statement.
The monk's life of abnegation included fasting and simple living.
Her abnegation of luxury allowed her to save money for charity.

meaning: to stop a plan or activity before it is finished
examples: The mission had to be aborted due to unexpected technical problems.
They decided to abort the outdoor wedding when the storm approached.

meaning: to shorten a text by removing parts
examples: The publisher asked the author to abridge the novel for younger readers.
This abridged version of the dictionary fits easily in a backpack.
mnemonic: A-BRIDGE: BRIDGE too long, SHORTEN it

meaning: to officially end a law or agreement
examples: The new government moved to abrogate the unfair trade agreement.
Congress cannot abrogate the basic rights guaranteed by the Constitution.

meaning: to leave secretly to avoid being caught
examples: The thief absconded with the money before anyone noticed it was missing.
He absconded from the country to avoid facing charges.
After confessing, he sought absolution from the priest.
The jury's verdict gave her absolution from all wrongdoing.
mnemonic: ABS-OLUTION: Like a solution for GUILT

meaning: to choose not to do something
examples: Many voters chose to abstain because they didn't support either candidate.
He decided to abstain from eating sugar for health reasons.
mnemonic: ABS-TAIN: Don't touch the TAIN(ted food)

examples: The professor's abstruse lecture left most students confused.
Quantum physics can seem abstruse to those without a science background.
mnemonic: AB-STRUSE: STRANGE and USEless = HARD to get

meaning: to agree to a request or demand
examples: After much persuasion, she finally acceded to their request.
The manager acceded to the employees' demand for better working conditions.
The dark frame accentuates the bright colors in the painting.
She wore red lipstick to accentuate her smile.
mnemonic: ACCENT-UATE: Put an ACCENT on it = STRESS it

meaning: easy to reach, get, or understand
examples: The new ramp makes the building accessible to people in wheelchairs.
The teacher made the complex topic accessible to all students.
mnemonic: ACCESS-IBLE: You can ACCESS it easily

examples: The young artist received critical acclaim for her first exhibition.
The movie won acclaim from audiences around the world.

meaning: an award or sign of high praise
examples: She received the highest accolade in her field for her research.
The team earned accolades for their outstanding performance.
mnemonic: AC-COLADE: A COLA celebration for AWARD

meaning: willing to help or please others
examples: The hotel staff was very accommodating when we requested a late checkout.
He's an accommodating neighbor who always lends a hand.
mnemonic: ACCOMMOD-ATE: Make room for others = HELPFUL

examples: The two countries signed an accord to reduce pollution.
They reached an accord after hours of negotiation.

meaning: to approach and speak to someone boldly
examples: A stranger accosted me on the street asking for money.
She was accosted by reporters as she left the courthouse.

meaning: slow growth by adding small amounts
examples: The cave formations grew through the slow accretion of minerals.
His wealth came from the accretion of small investments over decades.
Her acerbic comments hurt his feelings even though she meant to be funny.
The critic's acerbic review destroyed the play's reputation.
Though he disagreed, he acquiesced to the group's decision.
She acquiesced to their demands rather than start an argument.
The divorce was filled with acrimony and legal battles.
Despite their acrimony, they managed to work together professionally.
Her business acumen helped the company grow rapidly.
He showed great political acumen in navigating the difficult situation.

meaning: 1. very sharp or severe; 2. mentally sharp
examples: She felt an acute pain in her side and went to the doctor.
His acute observation skills made him an excellent detective.
She was adamant that she would not change her decision.
Despite the pressure, he remained adamant about his principles.
mnemonic: AD-AMANT: Adam was UNYIELDING in the garden

examples: She is adept at solving complex math problems.
He became adept at cooking after taking several classes.

meaning: 1. to stick to something; 2. to follow closely
examples: The label won't adhere to the wet surface.
All members must adhere to the organization's code of conduct.
The teacher admonished the students for talking during the test.
My parents admonished me to be more careful with my money.

meaning: to decorate or add beauty to
examples: They adorned the Christmas tree with lights and ornaments.
Flowers adorned the tables at the wedding reception.
The adroit magician fooled the entire audience with his tricks.
She handled the difficult situation with adroit diplomacy.
The celebrity grew tired of the constant adulation from fans.
He didn't deserve such adulation for such a small accomplishment.
mnemonic: AD-ULATION: ADULT fans give EXTREME praise

examples: The early chapters adumbrate the tragic ending of the novel.
His speech adumbrated major policy changes without giving details.
The team continued to play well despite adverse weather conditions.
The medication had adverse side effects that worried the doctor.
She advocates for better education funding in her community.
He is a strong advocate for environmental protection.

meaning: related to the air or sky
examples: The aerial view of the city showed its beautiful layout.
The gymnast performed an impressive aerial maneuver.
The aesthetic appeal of the building attracted many visitors.
She has a keen aesthetic sense and chooses beautiful decor.

meaning: friendly and easy to talk to
examples: The affable host made all the guests feel welcome.
His affable nature helps him make friends easily.
She has a natural affinity for languages and learned three quickly.
The two artists felt an immediate affinity when they met.
The affluent neighborhood had large houses and manicured lawns.
Despite becoming affluent, he continued to live modestly.
mnemonic: AFF-LUENT: FLUENT flow of money = RICH

examples: His rude comment was an affront to everyone in the room.
She took his criticism as a personal affront.

meaning: to make something seem greater or more powerful
examples: He tended to aggrandize his role in the project's success.
The dictator used propaganda to aggrandize his image.

meaning: 1. a total amount; 2. to gather together
examples: The aggregate score determined which team won the championship.
Data from multiple studies was aggregated to reach a conclusion.
The aggrieved employee filed a complaint with HR.
She felt aggrieved when her idea was used without credit.

meaning: able to move quickly and easily
examples: The agile athlete dodged defenders and scored the winning goal.
Even at 80, she remained agile and active.
He describes himself as agnostic because he sees evidence on both sides.
The agnostic approach encourages questioning rather than blind belief.
mnemonic: AG-NOSTIC: NOT knowing if God exists

meaning: farming; growing crops and raising animals
examples: Modern agriculture uses technology to increase crop yields.
The region's economy depends heavily on agriculture.

meaning: a walkway between rows of seats
examples: Please move to your seats and clear the aisle.
The bride walked down the aisle to begin the ceremony.
mnemonic: I-SLE: I walk down the aisle alone

examples: She accepted the job offer with alacrity.
The team responded with alacrity when help was needed.
mnemonic: ALACRITY: A LACR (like lacrosse) = FAST and EAGER

meaning: a false name used to hide identity
examples: The criminal used an alias to avoid detection by police.
Many writers publish under an alias to protect their privacy.
mnemonic: AL-IAS: I AS (someone else) = FALSE name

examples: The doctor's calm explanation helped allay the patient's fears.
A small payment was made to allay their concerns about the debt.
mnemonic: AL-LAY: LAY down your fears = EASE

examples: The news report alleged that the company had broken the law.
He alleged that someone had stolen his ideas, but provided no evidence.

meaning: to make pain or problems less severe
examples: This medicine will help alleviate your headache.
The new policy was designed to alleviate poverty in the region.

meaning: to set aside for a specific purpose
examples: The school allocated more funds to the science department.
We need to allocate time for each project carefully.
He seemed aloof at the party, standing alone in the corner.
Her aloof manner made it hard for new coworkers to approach her.
mnemonic: A-LOOF: LOOF (loofah) keeps you distant = RESERVED

examples: A minor altercation broke out between fans after the game.
They tried to resolve the issue without an altercation.
mnemonic: ALTER-CATION: ALTERcation changes friends to enemies

meaning: to combine or unite into one
examples: The two companies decided to amalgamate to increase their market share.
The recipe calls for you to amalgamate the dry ingredients first.

meaning: unclear or having more than one meaning
examples: His instructions were ambiguous, so we weren't sure what to do.
The ending of the movie was intentionally ambiguous.
She felt ambivalent about moving away from her family.
His ambivalent response made it hard to know if he agreed.
The new program was designed to ameliorate conditions in the shelter.
Regular exercise can ameliorate symptoms of depression.
He was amenable to changing the meeting time to accommodate everyone.
The company is amenable to negotiating a better deal.
mnemonic: AMEN-ABLE: Say AMEN = WILLING to agree

meaning: a feature that adds comfort or convenience
examples: The hotel offers amenities like a pool, gym, and free breakfast.
Good public transportation is an important amenity for city residents.
mnemonic: AMEN-ITY: AMEN! This COMFORT is nice

examples: The amiable shop owner always remembered his regular customers' names.
Despite the stress, she remained amiable throughout the day.
mnemonic: AM-I-ABLE: Am I able to be FRIENDLY? Yes!

examples: They reached an amicable settlement without going to court.
Despite the divorce, they maintained an amicable relationship for their children.
The amorous couple held hands as they walked through the park.
His amorous advances made her uncomfortable.

meaning: without a clear shape or form
examples: The clouds formed amorphous shapes that changed with the wind.
His plans for the project were still amorphous and needed more detail.

meaning: out of place in time; old-fashioned
examples: Using a typewriter in a modern office seems anachronistic.
The movie's costumes were anachronistic for the time period depicted.
The doctor prescribed an analgesic to help with the post-surgery pain.
Aspirin is a common analgesic available without a prescription.

meaning: similar in a way that allows comparison
examples: The human heart is analogous to a pump that circulates blood.
Learning a new language is analogous to learning to play an instrument.
The anarchist group protested against all forms of government control.
His anarchist beliefs led him to reject traditional political systems.

meaning: someone or something hated or cursed
examples: The idea of cheating was anathema to her strong moral code.
To the environmentalists, pollution was anathema.

meaning: a short, interesting story about a real event
examples: He began his speech with a funny anecdote about his childhood.
The book is full of anecdotes from the author's travels.
mnemonic: ANEC-DOTE: DOTE on a short STORY

meaning: loss of feeling, especially during surgery
examples: The patient was given anesthesia before the surgery began.
Local anesthesia numbs just the area being treated.
She cried in anguish when she heard the tragic news.
The injury caused him great physical anguish.
The animated discussion continued late into the night.
Her animated storytelling captivated the children.

meaning: 1. to add territory; 2. an attached room
examples: The country attempted to annex the neighboring territory by force.
We added an annex to the library to create more study space.
The court decided to annul the marriage due to fraud.
The new law will annul several outdated regulations.

meaning: something that doesn't fit the pattern
examples: The unusually warm December was an anomaly for this region.
Scientists are studying the anomaly in the data to understand its cause.
An anonymous donor gave a large sum to the charity.
She posted her question anonymously to protect her privacy.
There was clear antagonism between the two rival teams.
His antagonism toward the proposal made negotiations difficult.
The ancient Greek philosophers were antecedents to modern ethical thought.
Understanding the antecedents of the war helps explain current tensions.
His antediluvian views on gender roles offended many people.
The museum displayed antediluvian artifacts from early civilizations.

meaning: a collection of writings or songs
examples: The poetry anthology featured works from many different cultures.
The band released an anthology of their greatest hits.
She felt a strong antipathy toward dishonest people.
His antipathy for spicy food meant he avoided many cuisines.
The antiquated computer system slowed down the entire office.
Some antiquated laws still remain on the books.
The hospital rooms are kept antiseptic to prevent infections.
She applied an antiseptic to the cut to prevent infection.
His calm demeanor was the antithesis of his brother's explosive temper.
The peaceful protest was the antithesis of violent rebellion.

meaning: worry or nervousness about the future
examples: She felt anxiety before her important job interview.
The uncertain economic situation caused anxiety for many families.
Many young voters are apathetic about politics and don't vote.
His apathetic response to the crisis concerned his colleagues.
The apocryphal story about the president turned out to be a rumor.
Many apocryphal tales have been told about the famous explorer.
The appalling conditions in the factory led to new safety laws.
She was appalled by the amount of waste the event produced.

meaning: to calm someone by giving in
examples: The manager tried to appease the angry customer with a refund.
Giving in to bullies only serves to appease them temporarily.

meaning: to judge the value or quality of
examples: A professional was hired to appraise the value of the antique.
Teachers appraise student work to provide helpful feedback.
Police worked to apprehend the suspect before he could flee.
It took me a while to apprehend the complexity of the theory.
mnemonic: APPREH-END: END the chase = ARREST or UNDERSTAND

examples: The project received the approbation of the board of directors.
She sought her mentor's approbation before making the decision.

meaning: to take for one's own use
examples: The government can appropriate private land for public use with compensation.
He was accused of appropriating ideas from his colleague's work.

meaning: living in or related to water
examples: Dolphins are highly intelligent aquatic mammals.
The aquarium features aquatic plants from around the world.
The farmer searched for arable land to expand his operations.
Not all land is arable; some is too rocky or dry for farming.

meaning: a person who settles a dispute
examples: The union and management agreed to let an arbiter decide the contract terms.
As team captain, she often served as an arbiter in player disputes.

meaning: based on random choice, not reason
examples: The rules seemed arbitrary and unfair to the students.
His arbitrary decisions made it hard for the team to plan ahead.

meaning: the process of settling a dispute
examples: The labor dispute went to arbitration when negotiations failed.
Many contracts include a clause requiring arbitration before lawsuits.
Monkeys are arboreal animals that spend most of their time in trees.
The arboreal canopy provides habitat for many species.

meaning: known or understood by only a few
examples: The ancient text contained arcane knowledge that few could interpret.
Computer programming can seem arcane to those unfamiliar with it.

meaning: very old; no longer in common use
examples: Shakespeare's plays contain archaic words that modern readers may not understand.
The museum displayed archaic tools used by early humans.

meaning: a perfect example of a type
examples: Sherlock Holmes is the archetypal detective in literature.
Her story follows the archetypal hero's journey.
She pursued her research with great ardor and dedication.
His ardor for the cause inspired others to join the movement.
The arid desert receives very little rainfall throughout the year.
Years of drought left the soil arid and difficult to farm.
mnemonic: AR-ID: ID (idiot) in the desert = DRY

examples: The dictator arrogated power to himself without democratic approval.
He arrogated the credit for the team's success to himself alone.

meaning: an object made by humans in the past
examples: Archaeologists discovered ancient artifacts at the dig site.
The museum's collection includes artifacts from many civilizations.
The artisan handcrafted each piece of furniture with care.
Local artisans sell their pottery and jewelry at the weekend market.
Investigators worked to ascertain the cause of the accident.
Please ascertain whether the meeting is still scheduled for tomorrow.
The monk lived an ascetic life with few possessions.
Her ascetic lifestyle meant she owned only what she truly needed.

meaning: to credit or assign a cause
examples: Historians ascribe the fall of Rome to multiple factors.
She ascribed her success to hard work and good mentors.
He cast aspersions on his opponent's character during the debate.
She refused to respond to the aspersions made about her work.

meaning: to have a strong desire to achieve
examples: Many young athletes aspire to compete in the Olympics.
She aspires to become a doctor and help underserved communities.
The soldiers assailed the enemy position at dawn.
Critics assailed the new policy as unfair and ineffective.
The teacher will assess each student's progress at the end of the term.
Experts were called in to assess the damage from the storm.
Her assiduous preparation paid off when she aced the exam.
The assiduous researcher spent years gathering data for the study.

meaning: to make a feeling less intense
examples: A kind word can assuage someone's hurt feelings.
The cool drink helped assuage his thirst after the long hike.
The astute investor recognized the opportunity early.
Her astute observations helped solve the mystery.

meaning: 1. a safe place; 2. a mental hospital
examples: The political refugee sought asylum in a neighboring country.
In the 19th century, people with mental illness were often placed in asylums.

meaning: to make up for a wrong done
examples: He tried to atone for his mistake by working extra hours.
Many religious traditions include practices to atone for sins.
mnemonic: AT-ONE: AT ONE with yourself = REPENT

meaning: to waste away from lack of use
examples: Muscles can atrophy if you don't exercise them regularly.
Without practice, his language skills began to atrophy.

meaning: to achieve or reach a goal
examples: With hard work, she attained her dream of becoming a scientist.
The company attained record profits this quarter.

meaning: 1. to credit to a cause; 2. a quality
examples: Scientists attribute climate change to human activities.
Patience is one of her best attributes as a teacher.
Snow in July is atypical for this region.
His atypical career path took him from engineering to art.

meaning: bold in a way that shocks others
examples: The audacious plan to climb the mountain in winter impressed everyone.
Her audacious fashion choices always turned heads.
The speaker's voice was barely audible over the crowd noise.
Please speak in an audible voice so everyone can hear you.
She took a second job to augment her income.
The new features augment the software's usefulness.
The sunny weather on opening day was an auspicious start to the festival.
Her first successful project was an auspicious beginning to her career.

meaning: very plain or strict in style
examples: The monk's austere room contained only a bed and a desk.
The building's austere design reflected the architect's minimalist philosophy.
His avarice led him to make unethical business decisions.
The story warns against the dangers of avarice and materialism.

meaning: to punish someone for a wrong
examples: He vowed to avenge his brother's death by bringing the killer to justice.
The hero sought to avenge the wrongs done to his people.
She has an aversion to heights and avoids tall buildings.
His aversion to conflict made him a peacemaker in the group.
